name: HMS Widgeon (L 62 / K 62)
events: 25 Sep 1939 HMS Tribune (Lt.Cdr. G.P.S. Davies, RN) conducted trials off Greenock. She was escorted by HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN). (1)

19 Oct 1939 Around 0345 A/19, Truant (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Peers, RN) departed Barrow-in-Furness for the Clyde. She arrived at Greenock around 1830 A/19. She was escorted by HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN). (2)
20 Oct 1939 Truant (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Peers, RN) conducted trials in Gare Loch during which she was escorted by HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN). (2)

21 Oct 1939 Truant (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Peers, RN) conducted trials in the Clyde area. These included full power trials. On completion of these trials she departed the Clyde to return to her builders yard at Barrow-in-Furness. She was escorted, during the entire day, by HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN). (2)

22 Oct 1939 Around 0810 A/22, Truant (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Peers, RN) arrived back at her builders yard at Barrow-in-Furness from trials in the Clyde area. Her escort, HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN), had parted company around 0610 A/22 off the Lightning Knoll buoy. HMS Widgeon then proceeded to Belfast where she arrived around 1245 A/22. (2)

20 Sep 1940 Around 1430 A/20, HMS Ursula (Lt.Cdr. W.A.K.N. Cavaye, RN) departed Sheerness for her 11th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel, in area D 4, due to the threat of the invasion of England by the Germans.

On departure from Sheerness HMS Swordfish (Lt. P.J. Cowell, RN), which was to proceed to Portsmouth was in company. The submarines were escorted by HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN). HMS Widgeon joined the submarines off the Medway gate at 1515 A/20.
Around 0515 A/21, HMS Ursula parted company to proceed on patrol. (3)
This convoy departed Southend around 1000 A/2.

It was made up of the following merchant vessels; Alice Marie (British, 2206 GRT, built 1920), Chatwood (British, 2768 GRT, built 1929), Corcrest (British, 2373 GRT, built 1918), Corfirth (British, 1803 GRT, built 1934), Cormorant (British, 1220 GRT, built 1927), Dagenham (British, 2178 GRT, built 1919), Marlwood (British, 1034 GRT, built 1924), Pitwines (British, 889 GRT, built 1923), S.N.A. 8 (British (former French), 2569 GRT, built 1930), Sandenburgh (Dutch, 1013 GRT, built 1915), Sheaf Water (British, 2730 GRT, built 1925), Southwood (British, 1149 GRT, built 1937), Veerhaven (Dutch, 5291 GRT, built 1930) and Zelo (British, 2294 GRT, built 1921).
The Royal Fleet Auxiliary (RFA) tanker Larchol (1073 GRT, built 1917) was also part of the convoy.
On depature from Southend the convoy was escorted by the escort destroyers HMS Wolsey (Lt.Cdr. C.H. Campbell, DSC, RN) and HMS Valorous (Lt.Cdr. E. Mack, RN).

At 1600 A/2, the Larchol was detached to Harwich while the submarine HMS Triumph (Lt.Cdr. W.J.W. Woods, RN) and her escort, the patrol vessel HMS Widgeon (Lt.Cdr. R. Frederick, RN), joined coming from Harwich.
At 0437 A/3, in position 53°05'N, 01°35'E, HMS Wolsey collided with an unknown trawler. There was only some very slight damage done to HMS Wolsey and apparently none to the trawler.
At 0921 A/3, the Pitwines was ordered to proceed to the Humber due to insufficient speed. She was to join a slower convoy later.

At 1020 A/3, the following merchant vessels joined coming from the Humber; Clan Lamont (British, 7673 GRT, built 1939) and Helmspey (British, 4764 GRT, built 1931) while the Marlwood was detached to the Humber.
At 1355 A/3, HMS Widgeon was detached to join southbound convoy FS 98 / 3rd series.
The Sandenburgh was detached to the Tees for Middlesbrough.
The Corfirth was detached to Hartlepool.
The Chatwood, Corcrest and S.N.A. 8 were detached to Blyth.
The Cormount, Sheaf Water, Southwood, Veerhaven and Zelo were detached to the Tyne.
The remainder of the convoy arrived off Methil around 1000 A/4. (4)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
